review_text: Since Pokemon is exclusive to Nintendo, I picked this up instead. I enjoy pokemon, dont get me wrong, however with the price of a switch and the games I cannot afford it. However this game is a lot different as well, and has things that pokemon does not have to offer. I recommend this to anyone who likes RPG open world games. One other thing I would like to add is the price is not out the roof for this title. I got it and have been enjoying it as much as some of these 80 dollar games out there, and would argue this is remarkably better.

Good game all in all, bit rough around the edges but that's fine. Only thing that was getting annoying was that assigning summoned Pal to a task is very tedious now after Nintendon't said that it's a no-no to throw a spherical object in anything else than their own games. Luckily mod support fixes that and now I can merrily toss my spheres over the walls once again to defend my base or toss a Jetragon into an enemy camp and nuke it. Or when a buddy asks for more crafting speed I can toss my Pal across the base to the task.
Also the grind gets very tedious after around level 50, exp gain feels minuscule and every bit of new stuff uses loads of new materials that take ages to gather and craft.

[h1]After Being 9 Hours Deep[/h1]
I originally played 8 hours on release day and returning for 1 hour as part of my Steam Roulette series.

I was accomplishing a lot really quickly, constantly getting that dopamine hit, and that’s why I couldn’t stop playing.

I think it’s important to note that the game sells a "Pokémon with guns" vibe, but there’s so much more to it than that. It doesn’t feel like a Pokémon game. Yes, there are cute creatures, and yes, you’ll be unaliving those creatures a lot for resources. You’ll also use them to battle and to help around your base.

What stood out to me is the automation. There’s a lot happening where you feel like you’re making progress even when you’re not actively doing anything. It genuinely makes you feel like the boss, like a mafia boss controlling all the Pals you’ve enslaved.

[h1]The Good[/h1]
[list]
[*][b]The crafting survival loop is here[/b] - you’ll never feel like you’re searching for something to do
[*]The game constantly pushes you forward with quests, crafting, capturing new Pals, and exploring
[*]It never feels overwhelming despite having a lot of options
[*][b]Automation is a huge highlight[/b] - your Pals handle tasks while you focus on other things
[/list]

[h1]The Not So Good[/h1]
[list]
[*][b]This is clearly early access[/b]
[*]Pals glitching out and rubber banding
[*]There is a grind element that won’t be for everyone
[*]No real story to get attached to
[*]Very violent - I think this just might straight up turn off some players lol
[*]Text overlapping during gameplay
[/list]

[h1]TLDR[/h1]
This is not Pokémon. This is a crafting survival game where you put your minions to work, and even in early access, it’s incredibly addictive.

[h1]Verdict[/h1]
☐ Skip it
☐ Buy it on sale
☑ Worth it at full price

If I had to compare Palworld to Pokémon, Palworld is better in most ways but it has big problems.

For a "Pokémon knock-off", this is heavily overpriced and not worth that amount. If it was +-10€, even if 15€, I wouldn't say a word.
The game is enjoyable for few hours and then the will to play is gone for the rest of the game.
Imo survival and farming is a big miss, especially if the Pals, even if good are stupidly incapable of farming which make it then extremely annoying to even touch that feature again. 
Dying should be toggled off till Hard Difficulty.
Saddles are limited to few Pals, therefore you cannot even ride your favourite ones even if they could be rideable/flyable.
98% of quests are meaningless and won't help you in anything.
I love however the world building in this game and I had a blast exploring.

I'd still rather stick to Pokémon than this in honesty.
